items.prompt: Under pure edge-weight affinity on the starter graph, the top score is…
items.explain: Heaviest edges are A–B and D–E at weight 5.

items.prompt: Shared-neighbor affinity can raise a pair’s score above its direct edge weight.
items.explain: Overlap through common neighbors is added to the direct weight.

items.prompt: On the starter graph, shared-neighbor+edge score for A–B is…
Why does A–C rise under shared-neighbor scoring?
choices: Because A–C is the heaviest direct edge
Because A and C share neighbor B with a strong path
items.explain: min(A–B,C–B)=4 boosts the weak A–C edge.
